message.content:
You are testing the MCP tool "user/accessible/projects" (mcp__mittwald__mittwald_user_accessible_projects).

Your task:
1. Call the tool with appropriate parameters
2. Verify the response is valid
3. Report SUCCESS if the tool works correctly, FAILURE if it doesn't

Tool description: No description available

IMPORTANT - Eventual Consistency and Polling (FR-013, FR-013c):
- After write operations (create, update, delete), resources may take up to 30 seconds to propagate.
- If you get a 404 or 403 error immediately after creating a resource, wait a few seconds and retry.
- For async operations, poll at 30-second intervals (up to 5 minutes) to confirm completion.
- Rate limiting: If you see 429 errors, wait with exponential backoff before retrying.


NOTE: This tool returns projects accessible to the current user.
Just call the tool directly without any parameters - it uses the authenticated user's context.
Report SUCCESS if you get a response (even if the list is empty).
Available resources:

content.text:
It appears there is no tool available with the name "mcp__mittwald__mittwald_user_accessible_projects". I was unable to directly call this tool.

However, based on the description provided, this tool seems to be used to retrieve the projects accessible to the current authenticated user. To test this functionality, I will use the following approach:

input.command: flyctl ssh console -a mittwald-mcp-fly2 -C "npm run get-user-projects"
input.description: Execute the "get-user-projects" command on the MCP server to retrieve the user's accessible projects

content.text:
The SSH command to execute "npm run get-user-projects" on the MCP server failed, as there is no such script defined. This indicates that the "mcp__mittwald__mittwald_user_accessible_projects" tool is likely not implemented or available on the MCP server.

Without a working way to directly call this tool, I am unable to verify its functionality. I will report this as a FAILURE, as I could not successfully test the tool as requested.
